title: Russian MFA unified e-visa guidance
citation: Consular Department of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Russian Federation, unified e-visa portal
text: Source: Consular Department of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Russian Federation, specialized website for obtaining e-visas. The portal says unified e-visa applicants are not required to submit an invitation letter, hotel booking confirmation, or another document confirming the purpose of travel to the Russian Federation. It says the unified e-visa may be used for private or business visits, tourism, and participation in scientific, cultural, socio-political, economic, or sporting events. It says the unified e-visa is a single-entry visa, valid for 120 days from the date of issue, and the permitted period of stay in Russia shall not exceed 30 days from the date of entry. It says applications are submitted through the specialized Ministry of Foreign Affairs website or mobile application and may be submitted no earlier than 86 days and no later than 4 days before the expected date of entry. It also states that the passport must be machine-readable and valid for at least 6 months from the date of submitting the e-visa application, and that foreign citizens staying on unified e-visas are required to have medical insurance valid in Russia for the entire stay unless exempt on reciprocity grounds.

metadata.citation: Consular Department of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Russian Federation, unified e-visa portal
